s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: The Weiss Total Protection Package (Weiss Risk Ratings + Early Warning System + Windfall Strategy): Three-part protection system: 1) WEISS RISK RATINGS - 100% objective ratings on 7,000 stocks and 9,500 mutual funds. Unlike Wall Street analysts paid by companies they rate, Weiss accepts ZERO money from rated companies (only income from investors). GAO study confirms Weiss beats competitors 3-7x in forecasting failures. Gave Enron 'D' rating before collapse. 2) WEISS EARLY WARNING SYSTEM - Shows total market risk by aggregating individual stock ratings. Right now shows MORE risk than before 1987 crash or 2000 tech wreck. 55% of all stocks at extreme risk. 3) WEISS WINDFALL STRATEGY - Specific investments (put options) that soar when stocks crash. Turned $500 into $100,000+ in 1929-32 (father's story), $25,000 into $1.1M in 1987 crash (4,592%), up to 929% in 2000-2001 tech wreck.

Weiss, Ph.D. - Investor advocate par-excellence, economist extraordinaire and world-beating investment analyst - who publicly forecast the great Internet crash five months in advance and helped his clients multiply their money by 211%... 5000%... up to 9290% and MORE even while Internet stocks were cratering.

promise_architecture.promise_repetition: Heavy - '929%' appears in headline and throughout, 'turn tables on Wall Street' repeated, 'total protection' emphasized
promise_architecture.failure_reframe: You didn't lose money because you're a bad investor - Wall Street deliberately defrauded you with fake 'buy' ratings while privately calling the same stocks 'cr*p'. Mary Meeker made $15M telling you to buy stocks that crashed 98%. SEC is NOW nailing them for this fraud. Your instinct that something was wrong was RIGHT.

three_part_structure.what_they_think_problem_is: Stock analysts sometimes make mistakes and have conflicts of interest, but their research is generally trying to help investors make good decisions even if they're not always right
three_part_structure.what_real_problem_is: Wall Street analysts are PAID to lie to you. They give 'BUY' ratings to garbage stocks because their employers (investment banks) make millions in underwriting fees from those companies. Morgan Stanley paid Mary Meeker $15M to give Yahoo, Priceline, and Amazon 'strong buy' ratings - all crashed 95-98%. Salomon paid Jack Grubman $25M to upgrade AT&T (getting Salomon the underwriting deal) then downgrade after fees collected - costing investors 50%. It's not conflicts of interest - it's organized fraud. SEC is now prosecuting Merrill Lynch and 9 other brokers for this scam. Meanwhile, they're STILL issuing tainted ratings and declaring 'recovery' to sucker you back in.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: You followed 'expert' advice from big-name analysts at prestigious firms (Merrill, Morgan Stanley, Salomon) and lost 90%+ because their ratings were sales pitches, not analysis. Mary Meeker's 'strong buy' on Priceline at $104 → crashed to $1.50 (98.5% loss). Her 'buys' on Yahoo, Amazon, Drugstore.com all crashed 95-98%. Jack Grubman's 'buy' on AT&T Wireless → crashed 49.7%. His Winstar 'buy' → bankrupt, $3.1B investor loss. Every loss following their advice wasn't your failure - it was their paid deception. SEC confirms this with prosecutions.

villain_profile.primary_villain: Wall Street stock analysts paid millions to issue fraudulent ratings
villain_profile.villain_quote: Each one of Ms. Meeker's 'strong buys' was PAYING Ms. Meeker's employer - Morgan Stanley Dean Witter - to promote their shares! Morgan Stanley's underwriting department was paid millions for promoting Yahoo!, Drugstore.com and all the rest. And Morgan Stanley rewarded Ms. Meeker for helping them do it - with a mind-blowing $15 million paycheck!
villain_profile.secondary_villain: Investment banks (Morgan Stanley, Salomon Smith Barney, Merrill Lynch) and brokerage firms who profit from defrauding retail investors

The SEC is NOW prosecuting 10 major brokers for this fraud. Every loss you took following their advice wasn't your failure - it was their crime.

Your instinct that ratings couldn't be trusted was absolutely correct. The GAO study proves Weiss ratings are 3-7x more accurate because Weiss takes zero money from companies.

specimens.quote: THE GENERAL ACCOUNTING OFFICE of the US CONGRESS (GAO) heralded the fact that Weiss beat its closest competitor by a factor of 3 to 7 in forecasting future financial troubles. BARRON'S agrees, saying the GAO report is a 'glowing tribute to Weiss.'

Jack Anderson: 'THREE TIMES MORE ACCURATE'. Wall Street Journal: 'FEISTY'. Forbes: 'MR INDEPENDENCE'. NY Times: 'first to see the dangers and say so unambiguously'. Fortune: 'most comprehensive source'. Worth: 'Weiss' record is so good... nervous buyers need look no further.'

Mary Meeker issued 'buy' rating on Priceline.com at $104 per share. Within 21 months, the stock was 'toast' - selling for $1.50. If you had heeded Ms. Meeker's recommendation, you would have lost 98.5% of your money. She proceeded to issue 'buy' ratings on Yahoo (crashed 97%), Amazon (95%), Drugstore.com (98.9%), Homestore.com (95.5%).

Morgan Stanley rewarded Ms. Meeker for helping them do it - with a mind-blowing $15 million paycheck! Salomon paid Jack Grubman $25 million annual salary for issuing ratings that helped Salomon win underwriting deals.
Now, Merrill Lynch, and nine other big brokers are getting NAILED by the SEC - and yet they have the unmitigated GALL to offer MORE tainted advice.

Ours was the only firm in the world to issue low ratings - and specifically name - nearly every major company that later collapsed. We gave advance warnings about the failure of Bear Stearns, Lehman Brothers, Ford, General Motors, Fannie Mae, Wachovia, Citigroup, Bank of America, and many others.

One subscriber invested $9,000 in put options to sell IBM shares. On Tuesday, October 20, he got out with a clear $58,000 - a 544% profit in less than a week! Another subscriber said he bought stock index put options for $650 and sold them for $30,500 after Black Monday - a 4,592% profit.
CSFB Technology Index 929% in 42 Days, Gold 525% in 20 Days, Nasdaq-100 416% in 23 Days, S&P Retail Index 214% in 2 Days, Platinum 321% in 15 Days

Well before Enron failed, I nailed it with a 'D' rating - the equivalent of a junk bond rating. With special investments that help you profit when stocks decline, you could have earned a profit of up to 1,899% - enough to turn $25,000 into $499,750.

My Dad was 21 years old in 1930. He borrowed $500 from his mother and began shorting stocks on the New York Stock Exchange. By the time Wall Street finally hit bottom in 1932, Dad had turned his $500 into more than $100,000.

proof.strongest_proof_element: GAO (US Congress) study confirming Weiss beats all competitors 3-7x + specific smoking gun fraud evidence (Mary Meeker $15M for fake ratings on stocks that crashed 98%) + SEC prosecution of 10 major brokers ongoing

fssit_candidates.statement: Big brokers urged YOU to buy Priceline... Yahoo... Palm - and hundreds of other dogs while secretly telling favored clients that these stocks were really - and I quote: 'a piece of cr*p,' or worse!
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Names THE smoking gun that explains all your stock losses: Wall Street brokers were DELIBERATELY lying to you. They privately called the exact stocks they publicly recommended 'a piece of cr*p' while telling favored clients to avoid them. Every loss you took following their 'buy' ratings wasn't your failure - it was their fraud. The SEC is NOW prosecuting them for this. Your instinct that something was corrupt was absolutely correct.

fssit_candidates.statement: Each one of Ms. Meeker's 'strong buys' was PAYING Ms. Meeker's employer - Morgan Stanley Dean Witter - to promote their shares! Morgan Stanley rewarded Ms. Meeker for helping them do it - with a mind-blowing $15 million paycheck!
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Reveals the EXACT mechanism of Wall Street fraud: Analysts aren't giving opinions, they're selling ratings to companies for underwriting fees. Mary Meeker got $15M to issue fake 'strong buy' ratings. Jack Grubman got $25M. This explains why 'expert' recommendations from prestigious firms (Morgan Stanley, Salomon) caused 98% losses - the recommendations were PAID FOR by the companies. Every 'buy' rating was a sales pitch, not analysis.

fssit_candidates.statement: There is no charity on Wall Street. Most brokers and analysts are NOT in business to make you rich. They're in business to make themselves rich. And much of what they do and say - including their stock ratings - is part of a deliberate scheme to get YOU to do something that will make THEM richer.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Names the fundamental misunderstanding that cost you money: You thought brokers were on your side, trying to help you profit. They're not. They're in business to extract money FROM you. Every recommendation, every rating, every piece of 'advice' is designed to enrich them at your expense. This explains ALL your experiences with brokers - the high fees, the churning, the recommendations that lost money. It wasn't incompetence - it was their business model.

fssit_candidates.statement: THE GENERAL ACCOUNTING OFFICE of the US CONGRESS (GAO) heralded the fact that Weiss beat its closest competitor by a factor of 3 to 7 in forecasting future financial troubles.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Validates what you suspected but couldn't prove: Wall Street ratings are garbage. The US GOVERNMENT studied rating agencies and found Weiss is 3-7x more accurate than Wall Street competitors. This means every time you followed Moody's/S&P/broker ratings instead of independent sources, you were 3-7x more likely to lose money. Your losses weren't bad luck - they were the predictable result of following proven-inferior ratings.

fssit_candidates.statement: My Dad was 21 years old in 1930. He borrowed $500 from his mother and began shorting stocks. By the time Wall Street finally hit bottom in 1932, Dad had turned his $500 into more than $100,000. Some of the most famous investors of all time were wiped out. And there he was, in his early 20s, and he had become a very wealthy man.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Proves that market crashes are the GREATEST wealth-building opportunities, not disasters to fear. While famous investors were wiped out in 1929-32, Irving Weiss (age 21) turned $500 into $100,000 by shorting. This reframes your 2000-2008 losses: You weren't on the wrong side of bad luck - you were using the wrong strategy. Market crashes predictably transfer wealth from those long (following broker 'buy' ratings) to those short (following Weiss).

fssit_candidates.statement: Very few listened when we warned that Lehman would go belly up and that even the almighty Bank of America would come within an inch of its life. So I'm under no delusions here. I know that the vast majority of Americans will fail to heed this warning and fail to get ready for this crisis.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why you (and others) ignored previous warnings: Because 'prestigious' institutions like Lehman and Bank of America seemed invincible. Weiss warned specifically and publicly, but most people assumed 'too big to fail' protection. Your failure to act on warnings wasn't stupidity - it was normal human tendency to trust established institutions. This time you know better - Weiss was right before, prestigious firms were wrong.

rsf_analysis.fssit_placement_pattern: Front-loaded with smoking gun fraud quote, amplified with $15M/$25M analyst payoff reveals, reinforced with GAO 3-7x accuracy study, escalated with specific fraud examples (98% losses on Meeker recommendations), climaxed with 'they're doing it again' warning and windfall profit opportunity
